content: Fix the text wrapping in the TextBlock atom - content is truncating instead of wrapping at terminal width
Now migrate the chat views to use the component system. Start with chat/view.go

Reading the existing view code first.

The migration touches 4 files. Here's what I changed:

**`chat/view.go`** — 3 functions migrated:

`renderHeader` now uses `molecules.Header`:
```go
func (m *Model) renderHeader() string {
    ctx := atoms.DefaultContext(m.width)
    badges := []atoms.BadgeData{{
        Label:   "agent: " + m.agentName,
        Style:   theme.Style{Category: theme.CatAgent},
        Variant: atoms.BadgeOutline,
    }}
    return molecules.Header(ctx, molecules.HeaderData{
        Title: "CHAT", Badges: badges,
    })
}
```

`renderMessage` now uses `molecules.MessageBlock` for user/system, and `atoms.Badge` + `ui.RenderMarkdown` for assistant (since MessageBlock doesn't handle markdown yet):
```diff
-func renderMessage(msg Message, width int) string {
-    var style lipgloss.Style
-    label := ""
-    switch msg.Role {
-    case RoleUser:
-        style = ui.Dim
-        label = ui.Dim.Render("you: ")
-    case RoleAssistant:
-        label = ui.Accent.Render("assistant: ")
-    }
-    return label + style.Render(msg.Content)
+func renderMessage(msg Message, width int) string {
+    ctx := atoms.DefaultContext(width)
+    switch msg.Role {
+    case RoleUser:
+        return molecules.MessageBlock(ctx, molecules.MessageBlockData{
+            Role: atoms.RoleUser, Content: msg.Content,
+        })
+    case RoleAssistant:
+        badge := atoms.Badge(ctx, atoms.BadgeData{
+            Label: "assistant",
+            Style: theme.Style{Category: theme.CatAgent},
+            Variant: atoms.BadgeFilled,
+        })
+        rendered := ui.RenderMarkdown(msg.Content, width-4)
+        return badge + "\n  " + rendered
```

**`app/model.go`** — `renderStatus` migrated to `molecules.StatusBar` with health state mapping from `service.ServiceStatus`.

All existing tests pass. The CLI compiles and renders the same content through the new component pipeline.
Show me the current stack status

Here's the stack:

```
Stack: cli-components (on branch 3 of 3)

 ↑   main
   dugshub/cli-components/

 #   Branch                  Status
 1   1-core-atoms            ✓ committed
 2   2-chat-molecules        ✓ committed
 3   3-chat-view-migration   ✓ committed    ← you are here
```

**Branch 1 — Core Atoms** (15 files, 1243 lines)
- `atoms.go` — RenderContext, Role type
- `textblock.go` — styled text with theme token resolution
- `codeblock.go` — syntax blocks with language labels, line numbers
- `badge.go` — filled/outline label variants
- `icon.go` — 9 semantic glyphs
- `separator.go` — full-width horizontal rules
- 28 tests covering all atoms

**Branch 2 — Chat Molecules** (9 files, 518 lines)
- `messageblock.go` — role badge + indented content
- `header.go` — title + badges + separator
- `statusbar.go` — hints + health dot + service name
- `errorblock.go` — error badge + message + suggestions
- 22 tests covering all molecules

**Branch 3 — View Migration** (4 files changed)
- `chat/view.go` — renderMessage, renderHeader, renderPrompt migrated
- `app/model.go` — renderStatus, viewAgentSelect migrated
- `chat/picker.go` — migrated to atoms/molecules
- Demo mode with JSON script replay

All 3 branches pass `go build && go vet && go test`.
